title: Scan4D Stitching Manifest JSON Schema
description: Per-location manifest of spatial boundary links between scans, written to Documents/Scans/{locationId}/stitching.json and bundled into the Scan4D export zip. Each link pairs a boundary anchor in a source scan (Pin A) with one in a target scan (Pin B); final alignment is a server-side concern.

layout.description: Serialization convention for the 4x4 transforms in this manifest. Currently always 'column_major' (each inner array is one column), matching transforms.json / scan4d_metadata.json.

links.description: All spatial links whose TARGET scan lives in this location. Append-only; de-duplicated by link id.

id.description: Stable, persisted link identity (graph edge + de-dup key).

sourceLocationId.description: Location of the original scan that was extended FROM (where Pin A was dropped).

sourceAnchorId.description: UUID of the source boundary anchor (Pin A). Metadata-only camera-pose snapshot, not an ARAnchor.

sourceAnchorTransform.description: Camera-to-world pose of Pin A at the moment it was dropped, in the source scan's world frame.

sourceAnchorCompassHeading.description: True (or magnetic) compass heading in degrees [0,360) at Pin A, if available.

targetLocationId.description: Location of the new scan this link belongs to (THIS manifest's location).

targetScanId.description: The target scan (Pin B). Known only after the target scan is saved, which is why the link is written deferred.

targetAnchorId.description: UUID of the target boundary anchor (Pin B). Metadata-only camera-pose snapshot.

targetAnchorTransform.description: Camera-to-world pose of Pin B at the moment it was dropped, in the target scan's world frame.

targetAnchorCompassHeading.description: True (or magnetic) compass heading in degrees [0,360) at Pin B, if available.

linkedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the link was created.

linkType.description: 'mid_session' = Pin & Extend (boundary dropped during one continuous recording, then auto-saved + reset). 'cross_session' = Link Adjacent (boundary matched later by relocalizing against the source scan's world map).

matrix4x4.description: A 4x4 matrix as 4 inner arrays of 4 numbers each. Column-major per `layout`: inner array i is column i.
